disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The white lacy streaks inside the cheeks are completely painless and do not interfere with eating or drinking
trigger_question_examples: Do the white lines hurt at all?
Do they bother you when you eat or drink?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, they don't hurt at all. I only noticed them by chance in the mirror — I can eat and drink completely normally.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The throat-tightness is a subjective sensation that came on with the panic; the patient can still swallow liquids and talk normally, with no true difficulty swallowing
trigger_question_examples: When your throat feels tight, can you still swallow water and your own saliva?
Are you able to talk in full sentences right now?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It feels tight, but honestly I can still swallow water and I'm talking to you fine. It started when I began to panic.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: There is no swelling of the lips, tongue or face, and no drooling or new noisy breathing
trigger_question_examples: Are your lips or tongue swelling at all?
Are you making any high-pitched or noisy sound when you breathe in?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, my lips and tongue look normal, nothing is swelling, and my breathing isn't noisy — it just feels tight from the panic.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The white streaks are on both inner cheeks in a similar, symmetric pattern and were likely present for a long time before being noticed today
trigger_question_examples: Are the white lines on one side or both cheeks?
Have you looked closely inside your mouth like this before?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: They're on both cheeks and look about the same on each side. I've just never really looked this closely before, so I don't know how long they've been there.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The white lines do not wipe or scrape off when rubbed
trigger_question_examples: If you gently rub the white area with a clean finger, does it wipe away?
Do the white lines come off if you brush or scrape them?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I tried rubbing them with my finger and my tongue and they don't come off — they're part of the surface.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: After searching the appearance online the patient became convinced it was cancer and has been compulsively re-checking the mouth with a flashlight
trigger_question_examples: What made you most worried when you saw this?
Have you been looking it up online?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I googled it and it looked just like pictures of mouth cancer, and now I can't stop checking my mouth with a torch every few minutes.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: There is a pounding, racing heart and fast breathing that began together with the fright of the discovery
trigger_question_examples: How is your heart and breathing right now?
When did the racing heart start?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: My heart is pounding and I'm breathing really fast — it all kicked off the moment I saw the white lines and started panicking.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: There is no pain, no bleeding, no open ulcers, no lumps, and no recent weight loss
trigger_question_examples: Are any of the white areas sore, bleeding, or ulcerated?
Have you noticed any lumps in your mouth or neck, or losing weight without trying?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No pain, no bleeding, no sores or lumps that I can feel, and I haven't lost any weight.
